ending: The artifact thief's activation of Long Night stirs the Night's Shadow and leaves part of Arbitage transformed into lethal Wailing Stones. Garina contains the greater danger while Noah and Mascot force one tendril to withdraw. Brayden evacuates the students, but Noah suffers lasting soul damage, Mascot is nearly exhausted, and the artifact thief remains free. Apostle pressure then draws Noah into Kyle's contest for a rank-six rune. Noah publicly destroys the prize, secretly recovering its four flawless rank-five components. Alice and Carmen agree to hide how completely he misled them and regard him as a future rival. Back in Arbitage, Noah rebuilds Unstable Pandemonium as the flawless Keystone Rune Unraveling Disruption and moves well past the midpoint to rank six. Moxie, Lee, the students, Tim, the repaired demons, and Garina remain allied with him. Fuyin's planned Inquisitor operation is next, while the artifact thief, Long Night, the Night's Shadow, the Truth Seekers, hostile Apostles, noble pressure on Isabel, and Spider's hidden growth within Noah's divided soul all remain unresolved.
in_short: Noah's false claim of noble traitors unexpectedly exposes the artifact thief's real operation just as the thief steals Long Night and escapes. Noah accelerates his mixed class's training, learns that personal patterns can create self-fragments, survives a Truth Seeker assault, and helps the group shape their souls. Garina's lethal instruction gives him an unprecedented full merge, then reveals to her alone that Spider is the sealed self carrying Noah's memories of the Line. The thief activates Long Night and stirs the Night's Shadow, devastating Arbitage. Noah, Mascot, Garina, Brayden, and Tim keep the group alive, though Noah's soul and Mascot are badly damaged. Kyle then forces Noah into an Apostle contest. Noah appears to destroy its rank-six prize but secretly steals four flawless rank-five runes. He finishes by remaking Unstable Pandemonium as the flawless Keystone Rune Unraveling Disruption and prepares to raid the Inquisitors with Fuyin.

recaps.text: Previously: Noah Vines built a life at Arbitage as Professor Vermil while also operating as the masked Spider. He protects a mixed class of human and demon students with Moxie and Lee, and his unusual powers include Sunder, a fragment associated with Renewal, resurrection through a gourd, and a sentient grimoire. His students include Isabel, Todd, Emily, James, and Alexandra, while Sticky and other demons have joined the group despite the danger posed by the Inquisition. Isabel's master rune has made her a target of noble interests. Noah has also reached rank five and carries a powerful but unstable rune tied to chaos. The group enters this volume with divided identities to maintain, students to strengthen, and enemies among both noble houses and greater supernatural powers.

So far this book: Noah appears as Spider at an advanced-track meeting while Karina impersonates Vermil. Their invented story claims that traitors in the noble houses are seeking ancient weapons, driving the houses into conflict before they can unite against Isabel. The lie gains accidental credibility when Janice, a real covert observer, escapes Fuyin's sealed domain. Elsewhere, Janice's superior, the artifact thief, mortally wounds Jalen and steals Long Night. Jalen reaches rank seven, summons Garina, and helps her resist the thief, but the thief proves to be a hidden rank seven and escapes with the artifact. Imperial law forces the scarred but surviving Jalen to leave the Arbalest Empire. After Moxie warns Noah, he accelerates the training of his human and demon students. He tests his new rank-five power in the Scorched Acres and learns that its central effect is unmaking, though he still lacks reliable control and does not know how well it can affect magic or runes.

Noah begins teaching the mixed class how personal patterns support formations, insisting that the demon students start with safe, nonmagical practice. Alexandra's sword pattern draws energy from the world around her. When she completes the pattern's deeper meaning of freedom, it creates a Fragment of Alexandra and badly damages her soul. Noah stabilizes her with Renewal's fragment, and they conclude that a completed personal pattern can provide the intent, event, and energy needed to form a Fragment of Self without Sunder. Alexandra becomes a temporary teaching assistant so the others can pursue the discovery carefully. Noah develops his own musical pattern while three disguised Inquisitors attack him. His performance strips direction from their magic, lets him redirect their power through his unstable rank-five rune, and enables him to kill them despite their defenses. The creature manifested by his grimoire calls him a Herald, later accepts the name Grim, and defines the title only as someone who brings change. Its deeper meaning remains withheld.

Tillian, an Inquisitor of the Crimson Wake, asks a dangerous hidden figure for help investigating a surge of chaos connected to an old enemy. The figure travels to Arbitage with Tillian and a young Tauren trainee whose energy is exceptionally difficult to detect. He is revealed as Decras's expelled former eighth Apostle and has a hostile history with Garina. Garina nevertheless accepts his aid against the growing chaos problem. He leaves Tillian and the trainee under Noah's supervision, drawing Noah into the investigation. A demon student connected to the inquiry has disappeared because Jalen took her away while scouting beyond the Empire, although Noah does not know that. Noah trades limited information with Tillian and returns his attention to training. Moxie prepares to study Eternal Cycle within her mind space.

A coordinated cult attack strikes Noah's allies across Arbitage. The attackers call Spider a false Herald and claim their lethal tests will strengthen a true Herald. Moxie defeats intruders in her room, Yoru forms a moonlight arm to protect Tim, Mascot kills an attacker near Karina, and Alexandra survives a three-demon ambush. James uses layered deceptions to save himself and Emily, while Todd and Isabel defeat a team carrying a weapon designed to suppress their magic. Noah and Lee encounter an inner-circle cultist who neutralizes Noah's raw Unstable Pandemonium before escaping. Lee discovers that her own pattern can consume an object's detectable presence, including that of her recallable axe. Everyone in the core group survives, but the Truth Seekers possessed tailored intelligence and their source remains unknown. Grim confirms that Herald is a genuine title and that the cult considers Noah an unworthy claimant, yet refuses to explain further because the relevant words carry power. Noah resolves to refine his chaos rune instead of relying on force alone.

Garina takes Noah to an isolated training ground after learning his true name and demanding answers about his divine runes. Their exchange becomes a lethal spar intended to reveal the potential of Unstable Pandemonium. Noah learns to turn musical tones into tangible defenses, but Garina repeatedly kills him and studies the period after resurrection when his soul and body remain poorly connected. Exhaustion briefly aligns his mind, body, and soul. Garina then takes over his class and teaches soul shaping by driving everyone past physical and magical exhaustion. James learns that reshaping his soul already broke the rune oath he believed bound him to a trickster Apostle. Noah, Moxie, Lee, Todd, Isabel, Emily, Alexandra, Sticky, Violet, Yoru, and the established members of the group find individual routes to control their souls. The result allows them to hide their presence from domains. While their magic remains temporarily unavailable, Garina warns that Inquisitors are approaching.

Fuyin believes the Inquisition has been penetrated at its highest levels. Noah agrees to arrange a wary meeting with Spider and learns that noble houses have separately asked for Isabel's purge, though the Inquisition found no demonic influence. He repairs Violet and prepares the demons against anti-demon weapons. Garina resumes lethal training, forcing Noah to integrate his violin, pattern, body, and soul under constant pressure. Noah achieves a first full merge that lasts longer than expected, survives repeated deaths without losing it, steals power from Garina's runes, and briefly channels rune force even while his resurrected body is normally cut off from magic. He consciously remembers the entire state. With Noah's consent, Garina enters his soul and discovers the immense imprint of every step he endured on the Line. In its deepest layer she meets Spider, the sealed portion of Noah that carries those overwhelming memories and behaves as a separate self. Garina escapes and conceals the discovery, fearing that telling Noah would hasten Spider's emergence. Spider remains awake enough to study Noah's memories.

Noah completes the repair of his combat-ready demons with a method derived from Sticky, making them resistant to Inquisitor rosaries. The artifact thief activates part of Long Night and calls something ancient. The result stirs the Night's Shadow, a sealed peak rank-eight threat. Garina leaves to contain its strongest manifestation while Noah stays in the Scorched Acres to protect the retreating class. He uses his musical pattern and a formation to cancel the creature's soul-altering song. Mascot drops a mountain onto the descending tendril, giving Noah the opening to strike it with Sunder and force it back through its portal. The effort nearly exhausts Mascot, while Brayden spends most of his power evacuating the students. Noah revives with severe soul damage. Large portions of Arbitage have become lethal Wailing Stones, and the city's political order begins to collapse. The false Prophet increases pressure on Garina, while Decras discovers that the Apostle's original soul has been replaced. Noah's group prepares supplies, remains together, and plans for eventual escape, but an Apostle ferryman arrives to summon Noah before the Apostles.

The ferryman traps Noah in layered dreams and takes him to an Apostle gathering convened around Garina's conduct. Kyle reveals that the review is largely a pretext for a contest among Apostles' disciples. He threatens Noah into sabotaging Carmen so Alice can win a rank-six rune. Still weakened by the Night's Shadow, Noah enters the underground maze and bluffs Alice into treating him as a concealed expert even after she severs his arms. Carmen defeats Sin and allows Noah one token attack. Noah burns his body with Unstable Pandemonium, hides his soul from the contestants, reaches the bound prize, and shatters it with Sunder in full view of the Apostles. He lets the ruined body die after presenting the destruction as defiance of Kyle. The contest ends without a public winner. Sin reports that Noah's soul was immense, although he believes he could have killed Noah's body. Alice and Carmen agree to conceal how thoroughly Noah deceived them and begin treating him as a future rival. Noah awakens beside Moxie and reveals that the destruction concealed his theft of four rank-five constituent runes from the prize.

Noah begins repairing the soul damage left by the Night's Shadow and the contest. The four flawless runes taken from the shattered prize are Concentrated Singularity, Astral Ruin, Bleeding Night, and Creeping Death. He considers Creeping Death a better match for Moxie, leaves Astral Ruin intact for later testing, and postpones final decisions on the others. Rather than keep the flawed and overly broad Unstable Pandemonium, he uses Sunder to separate it and rebuilds its seven components around the narrower idea of directed disruption. The result is Unraveling Disruption, a flawless rank-five Keystone Rune. With an entirely flawless set of runes, Noah is now well over halfway to rank six. His soul is still cracked but usable, the Night's Shadow has only withdrawn, Mascot remains nearly spent, and the artifact thief is still free with Long Night. Garina continues to protect the group under hostile Apostle scrutiny, while Spider's growing awareness remains hidden from Noah. Noah's next immediate objective is the planned operation with Fuyin to raid the Inquisitors' runes.
